content.problemStatement:
**Please note that this problem contains subtasks.**

Given an integer NNN, generate a matrix AAA of size N×NN\times NN×N such that:


A(i,j)A_{(i,j)}A(i,j)​ is **distinct** and A(i,j)∈[1,N2]A_{(i,j)} \in [1, N^2]A(i,j)​∈[1,N2];
The **boundary sum** of all **square** sub-matrices **starting from A(1,1)A_{(1,1)}A(1,1)​** is **odd**.

For example, in a matrix AAA of size 3×33\times 33×3, the following sums should be odd:

A(1,1)A_{(1,1)}A(1,1)​;
A(1,1)+A(1,2)+A(2,1)+A(2,2)A_{(1,1)} + A_{(1,2)} + A_{(2,1)} + A_{(2,2)}A(1,1)​+A(1,2)​+A(2,1)​+A(2,2)​;
A(1,1)+A(1,2)+A(1,3)+A(2,1)+A(2,2)+A(2,3)+A(3,1)+A(3,2)+A(3,3)A_{(1,1)} + A_{(1,2)} + A_{(1,3)} + A_{(2,1)} + A_{(2,2)} + A_{(2,3)} + A_{(3,1)} + A_{(3,2)} + A_{(3,3)}A(1,1)​+A(1,2)​+A(1,3)​+A(2,1)​+A(2,2)​+A(2,3)​+A(3,1)​+A(3,2)​+A(3,3)​.


If no such matrix exists, print −1-1−1.

If multiple such matrices exist, you may print any.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single integer NNN — the length of the row and column.


Output Format
For each test case, output NNN lines where each line contains NNN space-separated integers denoting the matrix AAA.


If no such matrix exists, print −1-1−1.

If multiple such matrices exist, you may print any.
constraints: The sum of N2N^2N2 over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1062\cdot 10^62⋅106.
